chapter.title: Temporal Point Clouds
chapter.para: The pgPointCloud extension stores LIDAR point cloud data in PostgreSQL using two static types:

listitem.para: pcpoint — a single multi-dimensional point whose dimensions are described by a schema stored in the pointcloud_formats catalog table. A schema is identified by a pcid integer. Each schema fixes the dimensions present (X, Y, Z, Intensity, …), their numeric type, scale, and offset.
pcpatch — a compressed batch of pcpoint values that share the same pcid. Patches are the unit of storage and indexing.

chapter.para: MobilityDB lifts these types into the temporal world, so that a moving LIDAR sensor (a vehicle, drone, or hand-held device) can be represented as a sequence of pcpoint readings over time, or a stream of pcpatch snapshots over time. The lifted types follow MobilityDB's usual conventions for instants, sequences, and sequence sets.

A complete reference for the static pcpoint and pcpatch types is given in the pgPointCloud documentation. This chapter covers MobilityDB's additions on top of pgPointCloud: the set types, the bounding box, and the temporal types.
To use the types described here, both the pointcloud and mobilitydb extensions must be installed in the database, and MobilityDB must be built with the POINTCLOUD=ON CMake option:
chapter.programlisting: CREATE EXTENSION pointcloud; CREATE EXTENSION mobilitydb CASCADE;

sect1.title: Static Types pcpoint and pcpatch
sect1.para: The static types come from pgPointCloud and are documented exhaustively in its manual. We list here the accessors that MobilityDB adds or re-exposes under MobilityDB's standard naming, since they are needed to work with the temporal types.

sect2.title: Accessors

listitem.para: pcid(pcpoint) → integer, pcid(pcpatch) → integer — the schema identifier.
getX(pcpoint) → float, getY(pcpoint) → float, getZ(pcpoint) → float — extract a coordinate. The dimension must be present in the schema; otherwise the call returns NULL.
getDim(pcpoint, name) → float — extract any named dimension (e.g. 'Intensity').
SRID(pcpoint) → integer, SRID(pcpatch) → integer — the SRID declared by the schema.

sect1.title: Set Types pcpointset and pcpatchset
sect1.para: A pcpointset is an ordered set of distinct pcpoint values; a pcpatchset is the analogous set type for pcpatch. Both follow MobilityDB's general set semantics (see ): values are deduplicated on construction, the binary representation is canonical, and the usual set-level functions (numValues, memSize, asText, asBinary, asHexWKB, …) are available.
All values in a set must share the same pcid. Mixing schemas in a single set raises an error at construction time.
Set-level operators =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, @>, <@, &&, +, -, * are inherited from the generic set machinery and behave the same way as for the other set types.

sect1.title: Bounding Box tpcbox
sect1.para: The tpcbox type is the spatiotemporal bounding box for the temporal point-cloud types. It mirrors the structure of stbox (see ) — the same X/Y/Z/T axes, the same flag bits for which dimensions are present — and adds a single field: the pcid. Two tpcbox values with different pcid values are never considered to overlap, contain, equal, or be adjacent to each other, regardless of their geometric or temporal extent.

Carrying the pcid through bounding-box arithmetic guarantees that bounding-box pruning never drops a row whose schema differs from the query's schema, and never returns a hit across mismatched schemas.

listitem.para: tpcbox(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax, pcid, srid) → tpcbox — 2D box.
tpcbox_z(xmin, ymin, zmin, xmax, ymax, zmax, pcid, srid) → tpcbox — 3D box.
tpcbox_t(period, pcid) → tpcbox — time-only box.
tpcbox(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax, period, pcid, srid) → tpcbox, tpcbox_zt(...) — XYT and XYZT variants.
tpcbox(pcpoint) → tpcbox, tpcbox(pcpoint, period) → tpcbox — derive from a static point.
tpcbox(pcpatch) → tpcbox, tpcbox(pcpatch, period) → tpcbox — derive from a patch (uses the patch's embedded PCBOUNDS).

sect2.title: Accessors
sect2.para: hasX, hasZ, hasT, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, zmin, zmax, tmin, tmax, SRID, pcid. The min / max accessors return NULL when the corresponding dimension is absent.

sect2.title: Set Operations
sect2.para: The operators + (union) and * (intersection) combine two boxes that share the same pcid; mixing pcids raises an error.
sect2.programlisting: SELECT xmin(tpcbox(0, 0, 5, 5, 1) + tpcbox(3, 3, 10, 10, 1)); -- 0 SELECT xmin(tpcbox(0, 0, 5, 5, 1) * tpcbox(3, 3, 10, 10, 1)); -- 3

sect2.title: Topological Operators
sect2.para: The operators @> (contains), <@ (contained), && (overlaps), ~= (same), and -|- (adjacent) take two tpcbox values with matching pcid. The pcid mismatch always yields false (not an error) so that index scans behave intuitively.
sect2.programlisting: SELECT tpcbox(0, 0, 10, 10, 1) @> tpcbox(2, 2, 8, 8, 1); -- true SELECT tpcbox(0, 0, 5, 5, 1) && tpcbox(3, 3, 10, 10, 1); -- true SELECT tpcbox(0, 0, 5, 5, 1) && tpcbox(0, 0, 5, 5, 2); -- false (pcid mismatch)

sect1.title: Temporal Type tpcpoint
sect1.para: A tpcpoint is the lifting of pcpoint through MobilityDB's temporal machinery. It supports the same three subtypes as the other temporal types (instant, sequence, sequence set) and the standard interpolations (step / linear / discrete). All instants in a single tpcpoint value must share the same pcid; the constructor enforces this.
The bounding box of a tpcpoint is a tpcbox, computed at construction time from the X/Y/Z dimensions of the underlying pcpoint values together with the timestamps of the instants.

sect2.title: Constructors

listitem.para: tpcpoint(pcpoint, timestamptz) → tpcpoint — instant.
tpcpointSeq(tpcpoint[]) → tpcpoint, tpcpointSeq(tpcpoint[], interp) → tpcpoint — sequence from an array of instants. Interpolation is one of 'discrete', 'step', 'linear'.
tpcpointSeqSet(tpcpoint[]) → tpcpoint — sequence set from an array of sequences.

sect2.title: Accessors
sect2.para: All generic temporal accessors apply: numInstants, startInstant, endInstant, instantN, instants, startValue, endValue, getValues, numTimestamps, startTimestamp, endTimestamp, getTime, duration, memSize, interp. In addition:

listitem.para: pcid(tpcpoint) → integer — schema identifier shared by all instants.
SRID(tpcpoint) → integer — SRID inherited from the schema.

sect2.title: Per-Dimension Projections
sect2.para: A tpcpoint can be projected onto a single dimension to yield a tfloat. Three convenience helpers cover the spatial dimensions; the generic getDim accepts any dimension name from the schema.

sect2.title: Casts
sect2.para: A tpcpoint can be cast to tgeompoint, projecting away every non-spatial dimension while preserving the timestamps. The result has the same SRID as the schema; its dimensionality (2D vs. 3D) follows the presence of Z in the schema.
sect2.programlisting: SELECT ST_AsText(startValue( tpcpoint(PC_MakePoint(1, ARRAY[10.0, 20.0, 30.0]), '2024-01-01'::timestamptz)::tgeompoint)); -- POINT Z (10 20 30)
sect2.para: A reverse cast tgeompoint → tpcpoint is not provided, since reconstructing a pcpoint requires a target schema that the temporal value alone cannot supply.

sect2.title: Comparison and Ever / Always Operators
sect2.para: Standard B-tree comparison operators =, <>, <, <=, >, >= compare two tpcpoint values lexicographically over their canonical encodings. The ever-equal ?= and always-equal %= operators (and their ever_eq / always_eq functional forms) test value-vs-pcpoint membership.

sect2.title: Bounding-Box Operators
sect2.para: The @>, <@, &&, ~=, -|- operators between two tpcpoint values, or between a tpcpoint and a tpcbox, dispatch to the corresponding tpcbox predicates after extracting the bounding box. As with tpcbox, mismatched pcid always yields false.

sect2.title: Time Restrictions
sect2.para: The standard time restriction functions are available: atTime(tpcpoint, period), minusTime(tpcpoint, period), and the timestamp / timestampset / spanset variants.

sect1.title: Temporal Type tpcpatch
sect1.para: A tpcpatch is the lifting of pcpatch. It mirrors tpcpoint in every regard — same subtypes, same accessors, same casts and operators — except that each instant carries an entire compressed batch of points instead of a single point.

The bounding box of a tpcpatch is again a tpcbox; for each instant the box is grown to include the patch's embedded PCBOUNDS, so the box is computed in O(1) per instant rather than by walking each point.

sect2.title: Patch-Specific Accessors
sect2.para: In addition to all the generic temporal accessors, tpcpatch exposes per-instant patch metadata:

listitem.para: startNumPoints(tpcpatch) → integer, endNumPoints(tpcpatch) → integer — number of points in the first / last patch.

sect1.title: Indexes
sect1.para: B-tree (tpcpoint_btree_ops, tpcpatch_btree_ops, tpcbox_btree_ops) and hash (tpcpoint_hash_ops, tpcpatch_hash_ops) operator classes support equality and ordering predicates.

listitem.para: The pointcloud extension must exist in the database before mobilitydb is created. The temporal types reference pcpoint / pcpatch in their type definitions, so installing in the wrong order raises a missing-type error.

Schema mixing is rejected at construction time. A tpcpoint sequence whose instants have different pcid values cannot be built; the same constraint applies to pcpointset and pcpatchset. This restriction is intentional — the schema is what gives the dimensions their meaning, and silently mixing schemas would produce values whose coordinates are uninterpretable.
The POINTCLOUD=ON CMake flag is required. Builds without it produce a libMobilityDB that does not declare any of the types in this chapter; the SQL files for them are not generated.
